title: Honoring a Valid DNR Order During ICU Deterioration: DNR Does NOT Mean Do-Not-Treat
subtitle: A DNR (Do Not Resuscitate) order means no CPR — no chest compressions, defibrillation, or intubation — IF the patient arrests. It does NOT mean 'do not treat.' Patients who are DNR still receive active care consistent with their goals: oxygen, suctioning, antibiotics, IV fluids, comfort, and ICU monitoring. This case follows an ICU patient with a valid DNR (full treatment otherwise) who deteriorates from sepsis — the nurse must treat the reversible problem, verify the order's scope, support the family, and never abandon care.

patient.name: Eleanor Voss
patient.code: DNR/DNI (no CPR, defibrillation, or intubation at arrest) — FULL active treatment otherwise; valid signed POLST reflecting her own wishes
patient.admitDate: ICU admission for severe community-acquired pneumonia with sepsis. She has a valid, current DNR/DNI order and POLST that she completed herself while capacitated, specifying no resuscitation but full medical treatment otherwise. She now deteriorates — increasing work of breathing and falling oxygen saturation. The nurse must escalate appropriate treatment while honoring the DNR.
patient.room: ICU; on supplemental oxygen, IV antibiotics, and IV fluids; family at bedside

vitals.spo2: 86% — increased work of breathing, accessory muscle use
92% on high-flow O2 after airway suctioning
vitals.temp: 38.4°C; treated actively within DNR goals (no intubation)
37.9°C; family and patient reaffirm DNR with full non-resuscitative treatment
37.3°C; resting comfortably, care continued per goals

labs.r: Active infection still treated despite DNR.
Guides fluids/treatment within goals of care.

labs.v: Low with hypoxemia on the ABG.
labs.r: Treat with oxygen/suction; DNR does not bar oxygen.

labs.v: DNR = no CPR (compressions, defibrillation, intubation) at arrest. It is NOT 'do not treat.'
labs.r: Reversible problems are still treated per the patient's goals.

labs.v: Confirm the DNR is current, properly ordered, and matches the patient's wishes/POLST.
DNR is not the same as comfort-care-only; this patient has full treatment otherwise.
labs.r: Do not assume DNR means withdraw active care.

labs.v: Honoring a DNR is not abandonment — keep assessing, treating within goals, and supporting the family.
labs.r: The nurse continues full nursing care.

labs.v: The patient (or surrogate) may rescind the DNR at any time; reassess goals with changes.
labs.r: Document deterioration, interventions, and DNR status.

nursesNote: Eleanor Voss, 80, ICU for severe pneumonia + sepsis. Valid current DNR/DNI + POLST she completed herself: no CPR/defibrillation/intubation at arrest, but FULL active treatment otherwise. Deteriorating — RR 30, SpO2 86%, increased work of breathing. DNR ≠ do-not-treat: continue oxygen, suction, antibiotics, IV fluids, comfort, monitoring per goals; only resuscitation at arrest is withheld. Verify the order is current and clarify scope; treat the reversible problem; manage dyspnea; support and update the family; the patient may rescind anytime; document. Honoring DNR is not abandonment.
providerOrders: Code status: DNR/DNI (no CPR, defibrillation, or intubation at arrest); full active treatment otherwise
Titrate oxygen for hypoxia; suction airway PRN
Continue IV antibiotics for sepsis; IV fluids per protocol
Treat dyspnea/pain for comfort; continue ICU monitoring
Family updates; reaffirm goals of care; document deterioration and interventions

steps.title: Recognize Cues
steps.sub: What matters for this DNR patient's deterioration?
steps.inst: Select ALL items that are clinically significant.

opts.text: The DNR/POLST is valid, current, and reflects her own wishes
She is DNR but ordered full treatment otherwise (oxygen, antibiotics, fluids, ICU care)
opts.rat: DNR limits only resuscitation, not active treatment.

opts.text: She is deteriorating from sepsis — falling SpO2, rising RR, increased work of breathing
opts.rat: A reversible problem to treat within her goals.

opts.text: DNR applies only at arrest (no CPR/defibrillation/intubation), not to ongoing treatment
She may rescind the DNR at any time, and goals should be reassessed
DNR means withhold oxygen, antibiotics, and fluids and stop monitoring
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG — DNR does not mean do-not-treat.

opts.text: Because she is DNR, transfer her out of the ICU and give only comfort care
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG — she has full treatment otherwise; DNR ≠ comfort-care-only.

opts.text: DNR means no CPR, defibrillation, or intubation if the patient arrests
DNR does not mean 'do not treat'; reversible problems are still treated per goals
A valid DNR must be verified as current and consistent with the patient's wishes
The patient retains the right to rescind the DNR at any time
Honoring a DNR is not abandonment; the nurse keeps treating and supporting
A DNR order authorizes withholding antibiotics, oxygen, and routine care
opts.rat: ⚠️ WRONG — those treatments continue per goals.

opts.text: Treat the reversible deterioration within goals — oxygen, suction, antibiotics, fluids
opts.rat: #1 — She is not arrested; treat the hypoxia/sepsis now.

opts.text: Verify the DNR order is current and clarify its resuscitation scope
opts.rat: #2 — Confirm what is and isn't withheld.

opts.text: Manage symptoms and comfort (dyspnea, pain)
Communicate with the patient/family and reaffirm goals of care
Document the deterioration, interventions, and DNR status

steps.title: Generate Solutions
steps.sub: Provide or Withhold under this DNR?
steps.inst: Classify each intervention as Provide or Withhold for this DNR/DNI patient.

opts.text: Titrate supplemental oxygen for hypoxia
Suction the airway to clear secretions
Continue IV antibiotics for the infection
Administer ordered IV fluids
Reposition and provide comfort and skin care
Treat dyspnea and pain with ordered medication
Update and emotionally support the family
Begin chest compressions if she arrests
opts.rat: CPR is withheld under the DNR.

opts.text: Defibrillate/cardiovert a pulseless arrest
opts.rat: Resuscitation is withheld under the DNR.

opts.text: Intubate and mechanically ventilate at arrest

steps.title: Take Action
steps.inst: Rank the order of the nurse's actions. #1 = FIRST.

opts.text: Stay with the patient and assess airway, breathing, and circulation
Apply oxygen, suction, and deliver ordered treatments
opts.rat: #2 — Treat the reversible problem within goals.

opts.text: Verify the DNR order and clarify resuscitation scope with the team
opts.rat: #3 — Confirm what is withheld at arrest.

opts.text: Update and support the family and reaffirm goals of care
Document findings, interventions, and DNR status
The nurse gave oxygen, suctioned, and continued antibiotics/fluids while honoring the DNR
The nurse verified the DNR was current and clarified the DNR/DNI scope
The nurse treated her dyspnea, stayed with her, and supported the family
The nurse documented the deterioration, interventions, and that CPR would be withheld at arrest
The nurse withheld oxygen and antibiotics because the patient was DNR
The nurse called a full code and began compressions when she arrested
The nurse stopped all monitoring and assessment because 'she's DNR'
The nurse assumed DNR meant comfort-care-only and moved her out of the ICU without discussion
opts.rat: ⚠️ DNR is not comfort-care-only; she has full treatment otherwise.
